mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE stays below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) over 5-year out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index increase above 0.5% reflects accelerating manufacturing output that boosts near-term earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during high-inflation regimes or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in walk-forward validation across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 3.0% while the federal funds rate remains below 3.5%, real rates turn negative and force anticipated monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of triggered months or inflation-rate divergence shows no predictive alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly nonfarm payroll growth above 150,000 signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 150k correlates with negative equity returns or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth deceleration combined with falling real personal income indicates a demand contraction that reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP deceleration fails to predict negative returns when unemployment remains below 4.5% or signal accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside increasing housing starts reflects broad-based economic momentum that improves short-term market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined supply indicators show zero predictive power for 5-day returns or produce negative alpha in out-of-sample testing over rolling 2-year windows.
